members.member: Shift count and bit mask for A, R, G, B components in ARGB mode!

WARNING!!! WARNING!!! WARNING!!! WARNING!!! WARNING!!! WARNING!!! WARNING!!! WARNING!!! We can never change the layout of this class (adding or removing or changing the order of member variables) if you want to be compatible v1.0 version of the runtime. This is so that we can push into the runtime a custom marshaller for OLE_COLOR to Color.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IGraphics
member.summary: Encapsulates a GDI+ drawing surface.
member.remarks: Used to map a SkiaSharp graphics API with GDI+ API.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IBitmap
member.summary: Encapsulates a GDI+ bitmap, which consists of the pixel data for a graphics image and its attributes. A Bitmap is an object used to work with images defined by pixel data.
member.remarks: Used to map a SkiaSharp graphics API with GDI+ API.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IImage
member.summary: An Base base class that provides functionality for the Bitmap and Metafile descended classes.
member.remarks: Used to map a SkiaSharp graphics API with GDI+ API.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IFontExtension
member.summary: Extension class of Font.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IPen
member.summary: Defines an object used to draw lines and curves. This class cannot be inherited.
member.remarks: Used to map a SkiaSharp graphics API with GDI+ API.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IBrush
member.summary: Defines objects used to fill the interiors of graphical shapes such as rectangles, ellipses, pies, polygons, and paths.
member.remarks: Used to map a SkiaSharp graphics API with GDI+ API.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.ISolidBrush
member.summary: Defines a brush of a single color. Brushes are used to fill graphics shapes, such as rectangles, ellipses, pies, polygons, and paths. This class cannot be inherited.
member.remarks: Used to map a SkiaSharp graphics API with GDI+ API.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IHatchBrush
member.summary: Defines a rectangular brush with a hatch style, a foreground color, and a background color. This class cannot be inherited.
member.remarks: Used to map a SkiaSharp graphics API with GDI+ API.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IColorMatrix
member.summary: Defines a 5 x 5 matrix that contains the coordinates for the RGBAW space. Several methods of the ImageAttributes class adjust image colors by using a color matrix. This class cannot be inherited.
member.remarks: Used to map a SkiaSharp graphics API with GDI+ API.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IImageAttributes
member.summary: Contains information about how bitmap and metafile colors are manipulated during rendering.
member.remarks: Used to map a SkiaSharp graphics API with GDI+ API.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.ILinearGradientBrush
member.summary: Contains information about how Linear gradient brush during rendering.
member.remarks: Used to map a SkiaSharp graphics API with GDI+ API.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IColorBlend
member.summary: Contains information about how color blend during rendering.
member.remarks: Used to map a SkiaSharp graphics API with GDI+ API.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IGraphicsPath
member.summary: Represents a series of connected lines and curves.

member.summary: Represents the functionalities for using images in presentation

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Drawing.Image.Format
member.summary: Gets the image format. Read-only.
member.value: The image format.

example.code: 'Create an instance of PowerPoint presentation Dim presentation As IPresentation = Presentation.Create() 'Add a slide to the presentation Dim slide As ISlide = presentation.Slides.Add(SlideLayoutType.Blank) 'Create an instance for image as stream Dim imageStream As Stream = File.Open("Image.gif", FileMode.Open) 'Get the image from file path Dim image As Image = Image.FromStream(imageStream) 'Get the image format, read only Dim imageFormat As ImageFormat = image.Format 'Add the image to the slide by specifying position and size slide.Pictures.AddPicture(New MemoryStream(image.ImageData), 300, 270, 410, 250) 'Save the presentation presentation.Save("Output.pptx") 'close the presentation presentation.Close()

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Drawing.Image.Height
member.summary: Gets the height of the Image. Read-only.
member.value: The height.

example.code: 'Create an instance of PowerPoint presentation Dim presentation As IPresentation = Presentation.Create() 'Add a slide to the presentation Dim slide As ISlide = presentation.Slides.Add(SlideLayoutType.Blank) 'Create an instance for image as stream Dim imageStream As Stream = File.Open("Image.gif", FileMode.Open) 'Get the image from file path Dim image As Image = Image.FromStream(imageStream) 'Get the size of image, it is read only Dim Height As Integer = image.Height Dim width As Integer = image.Width 'Add the image to the slide by specifying position and size slide.Pictures.AddPicture(New MemoryStream(image.ImageData), 300, 270, 410, 250) 'Save the presentation presentation.Save("Output.pptx") 'close the presentation presentation.Close()

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Drawing.Fonts.FontManager
member.summary: Provides centralized management of shared font resources used across document conversion libraries (e.g., Word-to-PDF/Image, Excel-to-PDF/Image, PPTX-to-PDF/Image) and PDF processing.

remarks.para: maintains common font caches to optimize performance and reduce duplication. Font instances are cached to reuse during batch conversions and long-running services. Automatic cleanup is coordinated via a controlled by . When the delay elapses, unused or idle font objects are disposed and removed from caches. For deterministic cleanup, call to immediately purge all managed caches.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Drawing.Fonts.FontManager.pdfFontCache
member.summary: A cache for storing PdfFont, which is loaded from CustomFontSource and System fonts. Using a case-insensitive comparison for string keys.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Drawing.Fonts.FontManager.typeFaceCache
member.summary: A cache for storing SKTypeface, which is loaded from CustomFontSource and System fonts. Using a case-insensitive comparison for string keys.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Drawing.Fonts.FontManager.ttfFontCache
member.summary: A cache for storing TrueTypeFont, which is loaded from CustomFontSource and System fonts. Using a case-insensitive comparison for string keys.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Drawing.Fonts.FontManager.Delay
member.summary: Gets or sets the delay (in milliseconds) used to schedule disposal of font objects managed by .

remarks.para: When fonts are no longer referenced or marked as unused, an internal is configured with the specified value to trigger disposal and removal of those fonts from the caches.
The default delay is 30000 milliseconds, and the value must be between 1 and 2,147,483,647 milliseconds.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Drawing.Fonts.FontManager.PdfFontCache
member.summary: Gets the PdfFont Cache.
member.remarks: A cache for storing PdfFont, which is loaded from CustomFontSource and System fonts. Using a case-insensitive comparison for string keys.

member.remarks: A cache for storing TrueTypeFont, which is loaded from CustomFontSource and System fonts. Using a case-insensitive comparison for string keys.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Drawing.Fonts.FontManager.ClearCache
member.summary: Immediately clears all font caches managed by .

remarks.para: This method forcefully removes and disposes all font instances maintained in the shared caches. Use this to reclaim memory deterministically, instead of waiting for timeout defined in the Delay property.

This method can be called when shutting down an application domain or after completing a batch conversion. Even if this method is not called, the unused fonts in cache will be disposed one by one automatically after the timeout defined in the Delay property.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Compression.ArrayPool`1
member.summary: Provides a simple thread-safe pool for arrays of type . Arrays can be rented and returned to reduce allocations and improve performance.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Compression.ArrayPool`1.m_disposed
member.summary: Disposal flag for the pool instance. When true, the pool has been shut down and further operations (e.g., Rent/Return) should be ignored.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Compression.ArrayPool`1.Rent(System.Int32)
member.summary: Rents an array of at least the specified minimum length. The content of the rented array is NOT guaranteed to be zero-initialized.
member.param: The minimum desired length of the array.
member.returns: An array of type T.
member.exception: Thrown if minimumLength is negative.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Compression.ArrayPool`1.Return(`0[])
member.summary: Returns an array to the pool. The array should not be used after being returned.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Compression.Zip.GeneralPurposeBitFlags.SizeAfterData
member.summary: If this bit is set, the fields crc-32, compressed size and uncompressed size are set to zero in the local header. The correct values are put in the data descriptor immediately following the compressed data. (Note: PKZIP version 2.04g for DOS only recognizes this bit for method 8 compression, newer versions of PKZIP recognize this bit for any compression method.)

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Compression.Zip.GeneralPurposeBitFlags.Unicode
member.summary: Language encoding flag (EFS). If this bit is set, the filename and comment fields for this file must be encoded using UTF-8.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Compression.Zip.ZippedContentStream.Read(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Reads a sequence of bytes from the current stream and advances the position within the stream by the number of bytes read.
member.param: An array of bytes. When this method returns, the buffer contains the specified byte array with the values between offset and (offset + count - 1) replaced by the bytes read from the current source.
The zero-based byte offset in buffer at which to begin storing the data read from the current stream.
The maximum number of bytes to be read from the current stream.
member.returns: The total number of bytes read into the buffer. This can be less than the number of bytes requested if that many bytes are not currently available, or zero (0) if the end of the stream has been reached.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Compression.Zip.ZippedContentStream.Seek(System.Int64,System.IO.SeekOrigin)
member.summary: Sets the position within the current stream.
member.param: A byte offset relative to the origin parameter.
A value of type SeekOrigin indicating the reference point used to obtain the new position.
member.returns: The new position within the current stream.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Compression.Zip.ZippedContentStream.SetLength(System.Int64)
member.summary: Sets the length of the current stream.
member.param: The desired length of the current stream in bytes.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Compression.Zip.ZippedContentStream.Write(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Writes a sequence of bytes to the current stream and advances the current position within this stream by the number of bytes written.
member.param: An array of bytes. This method copies count bytes from buffer to the current stream.
The zero-based byte offset in buffer at which to begin copying bytes to the current stream.
The number of bytes to be written to the current stream.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Office.TTFFontStyle
member.summary: Specifies style information applied to text.

example.code: 'Create a new PDF document. Dim document As New PdfDocument() 'Add a page to the document. Dim page As PdfPage = document.Pages.Add() 'Create PDF graphics for the page. Dim graphics As PdfGraphics = page.Graphics 'Create a new PDF font instance. Dim font As PdfFont = New PdfStandardFont(PdfFontFamily.Helvetica, 12, PdfFontStyle.Bold) 'Draw string to PDF page. graphics.DrawString("Hello World!", font, PdfBrushes.Black, PointF.Empty) 'Save the document. document.Save("Output.pdf") 'Close the document. document.Close(True)

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Office.WordWrapType
member.summary: Specifies the types of text wrapping.

example.code: 'Create a new PDF document. Dim document As New PdfDocument() 'Add a page to the document. Dim page As PdfPage = document.Pages.Add() 'Create PDF graphics for the page. Dim graphics As PdfGraphics = page.Graphics 'Create a new PDF font instance. Dim font As PdfFont = New PdfStandardFont(PdfFontFamily.Helvetica, 12) 'Create a new PDF string format instance. Dim format As PdfStringFormat = New PdfStringFormat() 'Set the text alignment. format.Alignment = PdfTextAlignment.Center 'Set the line alignment. format.LineAlignment = PdfVerticalAlignment.Middle; 'Set character spacing. format.CharacterSpacing = 1 'Set clip path. format.ClipPath = True 'Set line limit. format.LineLimit = True 'Set line spacing. format.LineSpacing = 1.1F 'Set measure trailing spaces. format.MeasureTrailingSpaces = True 'Set no clip format.NoClip = True 'Set paragraph indent. format.ParagraphIndent = 2.1F 'Set right to left. format.RightToLeft = False 'Set word spacing. format.WordSpacing = 1.5F 'Set word wrap type. format.WordWrap = PdfWordWrapType.Word 'Set sub/super script type. format.SubSuperScript = PdfSubSuperScript.SubScript 'Draw string to PDF page. graphics.DrawString("Hello World!", font, PdfBrushes.Black, New RectangleF(0, 0, 200, 100), format); 'Save the document. document.Save("Output.pdf") 'Close the document. document.Close(True)

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.WordWrapType.None
member.summary: Text wrapping between lines when formatting within a rectangle is disabled.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.WordWrapType.Word
member.summary: Text is wrapped by words. If there is a word that is longer than bounds' width, this word is wrapped by characters.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.WordWrapType.WordOnly
member.summary: Text is wrapped by words. If there is a word that is longer than bounds' width, it won't be wrapped at all and the process will be finished.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.WordWrapType.Character
member.summary: Text is wrapped by characters. In this case the word at the end of the text line can be split.

example.code: 'Create a new PDF document. Dim document As New PdfDocument() 'Add a page to the document. Dim page As PdfPage = document.Pages.Add() 'Create PDF graphics for the page. Dim graphics As PdfGraphics = page.Graphics 'Create a new PDF font instance. Dim font As PdfFont = New PdfStandardFont(PdfFontFamily.Helvetica, 12) 'Create a new PDF string format instance. Dim format As PdfStringFormat = New PdfStringFormat() 'Set the text alignment. format.Alignment = PdfTextAlignment.Center 'Set the line alignment. format.LineAlignment = PdfVerticalAlignment.Middle; 'Set character spacing. format.CharacterSpacing = 1 'Set clip path. format.ClipPath = True 'Set line limit. format.LineLimit = True 'Set line spacing. format.LineSpacing = 1.1F 'Set measure trailing spaces. format.MeasureTrailingSpaces = True 'Set no clip format.NoClip = True 'Set paragraph indent. format.ParagraphIndent = 2.1F 'Set right to left. format.RightToLeft = False 'Set word spacing. format.WordSpacing = 1.5F 'Set word wrap type. format.WordWrap = PdfWordWrapType.Word 'Set sub/super script type. format.SubSuperScript = PdfSubSuperScript.SubScript 'Draw string to PDF page. graphics.DrawString("Hello World!", font, PdfBrushes.Black, New RectangleF(0, 0, 200, 100), format); 'Save the document. document.Save("Output.pdf") 'Close the document. document.Close(True)

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Office.FontDescriptorFlags
member.summary: Enumerator that implements font descriptor flags.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.FontDescriptorFlags.FixedPitch
member.summary: All glyphs have the same width (as opposed to proportional or variable-pitch fonts, which have different widths).

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.FontDescriptorFlags.Serif
member.summary: Glyphs have serifs, which are short strokes drawn at an angle on the top and bottom of glyph stems (as opposed to sans serif fonts, which do not).

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.FontDescriptorFlags.Symbolic
member.summary: Font contains glyphs outside the Adobe standard Latin character set. The flag and the nonsymbolic flag cannot both be set or both be clear.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.FontDescriptorFlags.Script
member.summary: Glyphs resemble cursive handwriting.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.FontDescriptorFlags.Nonsymbolic
member.summary: Font uses the Adobe standard Latin character set or a subset of it.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.FontDescriptorFlags.Italic
member.summary: Glyphs have dominant vertical strokes that are slanted.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.FontDescriptorFlags.ForceBold
member.summary: Bold font.

example.code: 'Create a new PDF document. Dim document As New PdfDocument() 'Add a page to the document. Dim page As PdfPage = document.Pages.Add() 'Create PDF graphics for the page. Dim graphics As PdfGraphics = page.Graphics 'Create a new PDF font instance. Dim font As PdfFont = New PdfStandardFont(PdfFontFamily.Helvetica, 12) 'Create a new PDF string format instance. Dim format As PdfStringFormat = New PdfStringFormat() 'Set the text alignment. format.Alignment = PdfTextAlignment.Center 'Set the line alignment. format.LineAlignment = PdfVerticalAlignment.Middle; 'Draw string to PDF page. graphics.DrawString("Hello World!", font, PdfBrushes.Black, New RectangleF(0, 0, 200, 100), format); 'Save the document. document.Save("Output.pdf") 'Close the document. document.Close(True)

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfMetrics.MacStyle
member.summary: Regular: 0 Bold: 1 Italic: 2 Bold Italic: 3 Bit 0- bold (if set to 1) Bit 1- italic (if set to 1) Bits 2-15- reserved (set to 0). NOTE: Note that macStyle bits must agree with the 'OS/2' table fsSelection bits. The fsSelection bits are used over the macStyle bits in Microsoft Windows. The PANOSE values and 'post' table values are ignored for determining bold or italic fonts.

member.summary: Created: International date (8-byte field).

member.summary: MagicNumber: Set to 0x5F0F3CF5.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHeadTable.CheckSumAdjustment
member.summary: CheckSumAdjustment: To compute: set it to 0, sum the entire font as ULONG, then store 0xB1B0AFBA - sum.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHeadTable.FontRevision
member.summary: FontRevision: Set by font manufacturer.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHeadTable.Version
member.summary: Table version number: 0x00010000 for version 1.0.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHeadTable.XMin
member.summary: Minimum x for all glyph bounding boxes.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHeadTable.YMin
member.summary: Minimum y for all glyph bounding boxes.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHeadTable.UnitsPerEm
member.summary: Valid range is from 16 to 16384.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHeadTable.YMax
member.summary: Maximum y for all glyph bounding boxes.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHeadTable.XMax
member.summary: Maximum x for all glyph bounding boxes.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHeadTable.MacStyle
member.summary: Regular: 0 Bold: 1 Italic: 2 Bold Italic: 3 Bit 0 - bold (if set to 1) Bit 1 - italic (if set to 1) Bits 2-15 - reserved (set to 0) NOTE: Note that macStyle bits must agree with the 'OS/2' table fsSelection bits. The fsSelection bits are used over the macStyle bits in Microsoft Windows. The PANOSE values and 'post' table values are ignored for determining bold or italic fonts.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHeadTable.Flags
member.summary: Bit 0 - baseline for font at y=0 Bit 1 - left SideBearing at x=0 Bit 2 - instructions may depend on point size Bit 3 - force ppem to integer values for all private scaler math; may use fractional ppem sizes if this bit is clear Bit 4 - instructions may alter advance width (the advance widths might not scale linearly) Note: All other bits must be zero.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHeadTable.LowestRecPPEM
member.summary: LowestRecPPEM: Smallest readable size in pixels.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHeadTable.FontDirectionHint
member.summary: FontDirectionHint: 0 Fully mixed directional glyphs 1 Only strongly left to right 2 Like 1 but also contains neutrals -1 Only strongly right to left -2 Like -1 but also contains neutrals.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHeadTable.IndexToLocFormat
member.summary: 0 for short offsets, 1 for long.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHeadTable.GlyphDataFormat
member.summary: 0 for current format.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHorizontalHeaderTable
member.summary: This table contains information for horizontal layout. The values in the minRightSidebearing, minLeftSideBearing, and xMaxExtent should be computed using only glyphs that have contours. Glyphs with no contours should be ignored for the purpose of these calculations. All reserved areas must be set to 0.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHorizontalHeaderTable.Version
member.summary: Version.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHorizontalHeaderTable.Ascender
member.summary: Typographic ascent.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHorizontalHeaderTable.AdvanceWidthMax
member.summary: Maximum advance width value in HTML table.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHorizontalHeaderTable.Descender
member.summary: Typographic descent.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHorizontalHeaderTable.NumberOfHMetrics
member.summary: Number of hMetric entries in HTML table; may be smaller than the total number of glyphs in the font.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHorizontalHeaderTable.LineGap
member.summary: Typographic line gap. Negative LineGap values are treated as DEF_TABLE_CHECKSUM in Windows 3.1, System 6, and System 7.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHorizontalHeaderTable.MinLeftSideBearing
member.summary: Minimum left SideBearing value in HTML table.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHorizontalHeaderTable.MinRightSideBearing
member.summary: Minimum right SideBearing value; calculated as Min(aw - lsb - (xMax - xMin)).

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHorizontalHeaderTable.XMaxExtent
member.summary: Max(lsb + (xMax - xMin)).

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHorizontalHeaderTable.CaretSlopeRise
member.summary: Used to calculate the slope of the cursor (rise/run); 1 for vertical.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHorizontalHeaderTable.CaretSlopeRun
member.summary: 0 for vertical.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfHorizontalHeaderTable.MetricDataFormat
member.summary: 0 for current format.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table
member.summary: The OS/2 table consists of a set of metrics that are required by Windows and OS/2.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.Version
member.summary: Struct field.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.XAvgCharWidth
member.summary: The Average Character Width parameter specifies the arithmetic average of the escapement (width) of all of the 26 lowercase letters a through z of the Latin alphabet and the space character. If any of the 26 lowercase letters are not present, this parameter should equal the weighted average of all glyphs in the font. For non-UGL (platform 3, encoding 0) fonts, use the unweighted average.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.UsWeightClass
member.summary: Indicates the visual weight (degree of blackness or thickness of strokes) of the characters in the font.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.UsWidthClass
member.summary: Indicates a relative change from the normal aspect ratio (width to height ratio) as specified by a font designer for the glyphs in a font.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.FsType
member.summary: Indicates font embedding licensing rights for the font. Embeddable fonts may be stored in a document. When a document with embedded fonts is opened on a system that does not have the font installed (the remote system), the embedded font may be loaded for temporary (and in some cases, permanent) use on that system by an embedding-aware application. Embedding licensing rights are granted by the vendor of the font.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.YSubscriptXSize
member.summary: The recommended horizontal size in font design units for subscripts for this font.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.YSubscriptYSize
member.summary: The recommended vertical size in font design units for subscripts for this font.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.YSubscriptXOffset
member.summary: The recommended horizontal offset in font design units for subscripts for this font.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.YSubscriptYOffset
member.summary: The recommended vertical offset in font design units from the baseline for subscripts for this font.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.ySuperscriptXSize
member.summary: The recommended horizontal size in font design units for superscripts for this font.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.YSuperscriptYSize
member.summary: The recommended vertical size in font design units for superscripts for this font.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.YSuperscriptXOffset
member.summary: The recommended horizontal offset in font design units for superscripts for this font.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.YSuperscriptYOffset
member.summary: The recommended vertical offset in font design units from the baseline for superscripts for this font.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.YStrikeoutSize
member.summary: Width of the strikeout stroke in font design units.

member.summary: The position of the strikeout stroke relative to the baseline in font design units.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.Panose
member.summary: This 10 byte series of numbers are used to describe the visual characteristics of a given typeface. These characteristics are then used to associate the font with other fonts of similar appearance having different names. The variables for each digit are listed below. The specifications for each variable can be obtained in the specification PANOSE v2.0 Numerical Evaluation from Microsoft or Elseware Corporation.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.UsFirstCharIndex
member.summary: The minimum Unicode index (character code) in this font, according to the cmap subtable for platform ID 3 and encoding ID 0 or 1. For most fonts supporting Win-ANSI or other character sets, this value would be 0x0020.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.UsLastCharIndex
member.summary: usLastCharIndex: The maximum Unicode index (character code) in this font, according to the cmap subtable for platform ID 3 and encoding ID 0 or 1. This value depends on which character sets the font supports.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.STypoAscender
member.summary: The typographic ascender for this font. Remember that this is not the same as the Ascender value in the 'hhea' table, which Apple defines in a far different manner. DEF_TABLE_OFFSET good source for usTypoAscender is the Ascender value from an AFM file.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.STypoDescender
member.summary: The typographic descender for this font. Remember that this is not the same as the Descender value in the 'hhea' table, which Apple defines in a far different manner. DEF_TABLE_OFFSET good source for usTypoDescender is the Descender value from an AFM file.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.STypoLineGap
member.summary: The typographic line gap for this font. Remember that this is not the same as the LineGap value in the 'hhea' table, which Apple defines in a far different manner.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.UsWinAscent
member.summary: The ascender metric for Windows. This too is distinct from Apple's Ascender value and from the usTypoAscender values. usWinAscent is computed as the yMax for all characters in the Windows ANSI character set. usTypoAscent is used to compute the Windows font height and default line spacing. For platform 3 encoding 0 fonts, it is the same as yMax.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.UsWinDescent
member.summary: The descender metric for Windows. This too is distinct from Apple's Descender value and from the usTypoDescender values. usWinDescent is computed as the -yMin for all characters in the Windows ANSI character set. usTypoAscent is used to compute the Windows font height and default line spacing. For platform 3 encoding 0 fonts, it is the same as -yMin.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.UlCodePageRange1
member.summary: This field is used to specify the code pages encompassed by the font file in the 'cmap' subtable for platform 3, encoding ID 1 (Microsoft platform). If the font file is encoding ID 0, then the Symbol Character Set bit should be set. If the bit is set (1) then the code page is considered functional. If the bit is clear (0) then the code page is not considered functional. Each of the bits is treated as an independent flag and the bits can be set in any combination. The determination of "functional" is left up to the font designer, although character set selection should attempt to be functional by code pages if at all possible.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.TtfOS2Table.UlCodePageRange2
member.summary: This field is used to specify the code pages encompassed by the font file in the 'cmap' subtable for platform 3, encoding ID 1 (Microsoft platform). If the font file is encoding ID 0, then the Symbol Character Set bit should be set. If the bit is set (1) then the code page is considered functional. If the bit is clear (0) then the code page is not considered functional. Each of the bits is treated as an independent flag and the bits can be set in any combination. The determination of "functional" is left up to the font designer, although character set selection should attempt to be functional by code pages if at all possible.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Office.Font
member.summary: Defines a particular format for text, including font face, size, and style attributes.

example.code: 'Create a new PDF document. Dim document As New PdfDocument() 'Add a page to the document. Dim page As PdfPage = document.Pages.Add() 'Create PDF graphics for the page. Dim graphics As PdfGraphics = page.Graphics 'Create a new PDF font instance. Dim font As PdfFont = New PdfStandardFont(PdfFontFamily.Helvetica, 12) 'Draw string to PDF page. graphics.DrawString("Hello World!", font, PdfBrushes.Black, PointF.Empty) 'Save the document. document.Save("Output.pdf") 'Close the document. document.Close(True)

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.Font.Name
member.summary: Gets the face name of this Font.
member.value: A string representation of the face name of this .

example.code: 'Create a new PDF document. Dim document As New PdfDocument() 'Add a page to the document. Dim page As PdfPage = document.Pages.Add() 'Create PDF graphics for the page. Dim graphics As PdfGraphics = page.Graphics 'Create a new PDF font instance. Dim font As PdfFont = New PdfStandardFont(PdfFontFamily.Helvetica, 12) 'Get font Name. Dim fontName As String = font.Name 'Get font size. Dim fontSize As Single = font.Size 'Get font height. Dim fontHeight As Single = font.Height 'Get the font style. Dim style As PdfFontStyle = font.Style Dim builder As New StringBuilder() builder.AppendLine("Font Name: " + fontName) builder.AppendLine("Font Size: " + fontSize) builder.AppendLine("Font Height: " + fontHeight) builder.AppendLine("Font Style: " + style.ToString()) 'Draw string to PDF page. graphics.DrawString(builder.ToString(), font, PdfBrushes.Black, PointF.Empty) 'Save the document. document.Save("Output.pdf") 'Close the document. document.Close(True)

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.Font.Bold
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this is bold.
member.value: true if bold; otherwise, false.

example.code: 'Create a new PDF document. Dim document As New PdfDocument() 'Add a page to the document. Dim page As PdfPage = document.Pages.Add() 'Create PDF graphics for the page. Dim graphics As PdfGraphics = page.Graphics 'Create a new PDF font instance. Dim font As PdfFont = New PdfStandardFont(PdfFontFamily.Helvetica, 12) 'Get font Name. Dim fontName As String = font.Name 'Get font size. Dim fontSize As Single = font.Size 'Get font height. Dim fontHeight As Single = font.Height 'Get the font style. Dim style As PdfFontStyle = font.Style Dim builder As New StringBuilder() builder.AppendLine("Font Name: " + fontName) builder.AppendLine("Font Size: " + fontSize) builder.AppendLine("Font Height: " + fontHeight) builder.AppendLine("Font Style: " + style.ToString()) builder.AppendLine("Bold: " + font.Bold.ToString()) builder.AppendLine("Italic: " + font.Italic.ToString()) builder.AppendLine("Underline: " + font.Underline.ToString()) builder.AppendLine("Strikeout: " + font.Strikeout.ToString()) 'Draw string to PDF page. graphics.DrawString(builder.ToString(), font, PdfBrushes.Black, PointF.Empty) 'Save the document. document.Save("Output.pdf") 'Close the document. document.Close(True)

example.code: 'Create a new PDF document. Dim document As New PdfDocument() 'Add a page to the document. Dim page As PdfPage = document.Pages.Add() 'Create PDF graphics for the page. Dim graphics As PdfGraphics = page.Graphics 'Create a new PDF font instance. Dim font As PdfFont = New PdfStandardFont(PdfFontFamily.Helvetica, 12) 'Get font Name. Dim fontName As String = font.Name 'Get font size. Dim fontSize As Single = font.Size 'Get font height. Dim fontHeight As Single = font.Height 'Get the font style. Dim style As PdfFontStyle = font.Style Dim builder As New StringBuilder() builder.AppendLine("Font Name: " + fontName) builder.AppendLine("Font Size: " + fontSize) builder.AppendLine("Font Height: " + fontHeight) builder.AppendLine("Font Style: " + style.ToString()) builder.AppendLine("Bold: " + font.Bold.ToString()) builder.AppendLine("Italic: " + font.Italic.ToString()) builder.AppendLine("Underline: " + font.Underline.ToString()) builder.AppendLine("Strikeout: " + font.Strikeout.ToString()) 'Draw string to PDF page. graphics.DrawString(builder.ToString(), font, PdfBrushes.Black, PointF.Empty) 'Save the document. document.Save("Output.pdf") 'Close the document. document.Close(True)

example.code: 'Create a new PDF document. Dim document As New PdfDocument() 'Add a page to the document. Dim page As PdfPage = document.Pages.Add() 'Create PDF graphics for the page. Dim graphics As PdfGraphics = page.Graphics 'Create a new PDF font instance. Dim font As PdfFont = New PdfStandardFont(PdfFontFamily.Helvetica, 12) 'Create a new PDF string format instance. Dim format As PdfStringFormat = New PdfStringFormat() 'Set the text alignment. format.Alignment = PdfTextAlignment.Center 'Draw string to PDF page. graphics.DrawString("Hello World!", font, PdfBrushes.Black, New RectangleF(0, 0, 200, 100), format); 'Save the document. document.Save("Output.pdf") 'Close the document. document.Close(True)

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.TrueTypeFontStringFormat.EnableBaseline
member.summary: Gets or sets the baseline for line alignment.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.TrueTypeFontStringFormat.RightToLeft
member.summary: Gets or sets the value that indicates text direction mode.
member.value: true if RightToLeft; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: Note, that this property doesn't change any alignment of the text. property should be set manually to align the text. This property just enables or disables support of right to left approach. If the value is False, the text won't be checked for right to left symbols occurrence. This property is not supported in WinRT, Windows Phone, Xamarin, Universal Windows Platform and Silverlight.

example.code: 'Create a new PDF document. Dim document As New PdfDocument() 'Add a page to the document. Dim page As PdfPage = document.Pages.Add() 'Create PDF graphics for the page. Dim format As PdfStringFormat = New PdfStringFormat() 'Set the text alignment. format.Alignment = PdfTextAlignment.Center 'Set the line alignment. format.LineAlignment = PdfVerticalAlignment.Middle; 'Set right to left. format.RightToLeft = False 'Draw string to PDF page. graphics.DrawString("Hello World!", font, PdfBrushes.Black, New RectangleF(0, 0, 200, 100), format); 'Save the document. document.Save("Output.pdf") 'Close the document. document.Close(True)

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.TrueTypeFontStringFormat.CharacterSpacing
member.summary: Gets or sets value that indicates a size among the characters in the text. When the glyph for each character in the string is rendered, this value is added to the glyph�s displacement.
member.remarks: Default value is 0.

example.code: 'Create a new PDF document. Dim document As New PdfDocument() 'Add a page to the document. Dim page As PdfPage = document.Pages.Add() 'Create PDF graphics for the page. Dim graphics As PdfGraphics = page.Graphics 'Create a new PDF font instance. Dim font As PdfFont = New PdfStandardFont(PdfFontFamily.Helvetica, 12) 'Create a new PDF string format instance. Dim format As PdfStringFormat = New PdfStringFormat() 'Set the text alignment. format.Alignment = PdfTextAlignment.Center 'Set the line alignment. format.LineAlignment = PdfVerticalAlignment.Middle; 'Set character spacing. format.CharacterSpacing = 1 'Draw string to PDF page. graphics.DrawString("Hello World!", font, PdfBrushes.Black, New RectangleF(0, 0, 200, 100), format); 'Save the document. document.Save("Output.pdf") 'Close the document. document.Close(True)

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.TrueTypeFontStringFormat.WordSpacing
member.summary: Gets or sets value that indicates a size among the words in the text. Word spacing works the same way as character spacing but applies only to the space character, code 32.
member.remarks: Default value is 0.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.TrueTypeFontStringFormat.LineSpacing
member.summary: Gets or sets value that indicates the vertical distance between the baselines of adjacent lines of text.
member.remarks: Default value is 0.

example.code: 'Create a new PDF document. Dim document As New PdfDocument() 'Add a page to the document. Dim page As PdfPage = document.Pages.Add() 'Create PDF graphics for the page. Dim graphics As PdfGraphics = page.Graphics 'Create new system font. Dim sFont As New Font("Arial", 12, FontStyle.Regular) 'Create a new PDF true type font instance. Dim font As PdfTrueTypeFont = New PdfTrueTypeFont(sFont, 12, True) 'Check unicode support. Dim isUnicode As Boolean = font.Unicode 'Draw string to PDF page. graphics.DrawString("Unicode Font = " + isUnicode, font, PdfBrushes.Black, PointF.Empty) 'Save the document. document.Save("Output.pdf") 'Close the document. document.Close(True)

member.param: Represent a start Unicode range of fallback font.

member.summary: Initializes a set of default fallback fonts.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Office.CharacterRangeType
member.summary: Used to represent the character range type of the text.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.TextComposer.CombineDiacriticMark(System.String,System.String,System.String[]@)
member.summary: Combines diacritic marks from consecutive text ranges.
member.param: The output strings after possible diacritic combination.
member.returns: True if diacritic marks were combined; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.TextComposer.CountDiacriticMarks(System.String)
member.summary: Counts the number of consecutive diacritic (combining mark) characters starting from the beginning of the given string.
member.returns: The count of leading diacritic marks at the start of the string.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.TextComposer.IsDiacriticMark(System.Char)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified character is a Unicode combining diacritic mark.
member.returns: True if the character is a nonspacing, spacing combining, or enclosing mark; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.TextComposer.IsBaseCharacter(System.Char)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified character is a "base character", i.e., a visual letter or meaningful root in a script (not a whitespace, punctuation, control, surrogate, digit, symbol, separator, or diacritic).
member.returns: True if the character is a base character; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.TextComposer.Normalize(System.String,System.String@)
member.summary: Performs Unicode normalization (Form C) to combine diacritic characters with their base character in the input string.
member.param: The normalized output text, combining diacritics with base characters if normalization succeeds; otherwise, the original input.
member.returns: True if normalization succeeded; otherwise, false.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Office.ImageRecolorer
member.summary: Provides image recoloring utilities such as grayscale, black-and-white, washout, and duotone effects similar to those available in Microsoft application.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.ImageRecolorer.ApplyGrayScaleEffect(Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IImage)
member.summary: Applies a grayscale effect to the given image.
member.param: The image to apply the grayscale effect on.
member.returns: A new image instance with the grayscale effect applied.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.ImageRecolorer.ApplyBlackAndWhiteEffect(Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IImage,System.Single)
member.summary: Applies a black and white effect to the given image based on a specified threshold.
member.param: The image to which the black and white effect will be applied.
The threshold value to determine the conversion to black or white.
member.returns: A new image instance with the black and white effect applied.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.ImageRecolorer.ApplyWashoutEffect(Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IImage,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Applies a washout effect to the specified image.
member.param: The image to which the washout effect will be applied.
member.returns: An object with the washout effect applied.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.ImageRecolorer.ApplyDueToneColors(Syncfusion.Drawing.DocIOHelper.IImage,Syncfusion.Drawing.Color,Syncfusion.Drawing.Color)
member.summary: Applies a duotone effect to the given image using the specified colors.
member.param: The original image to which the duotone effect will be applied.
First color used for the duotone effect.
Second color used for the duotone effect.
member.returns: A new image instance with the duotone effect applied.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Office.ICrop
member.summary: Represents the crop properties used to define the portion of an image to be cropped.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.ICrop.OffsetX
member.summary: Gets or sets the x-axis offset of the image to be cropped.
member.exception: Thrown if the specified value for is outside the valid range of -169092 to 169092.
member.remarks: The bounding box properties (, , , ) must be set before this cropping property for proper functionality.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.ICrop.OffsetY
member.summary: Gets or sets the y-axis offset of the image to be cropped.
member.exception: Thrown if the specified value for is outside the valid range of -169092 to 169092.
member.remarks: The bounding box properties (, , , ) must be set before this cropping property for proper functionality.

member.summary: Gets or sets the width of the image to be cropped.
member.exception: Thrown if the specified value for is outside the valid range of 0 to 169092.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.ICrop.Height
member.summary: Gets or sets the height of the image to be cropped.
member.exception: Thrown if the specified value for is outside the valid range of 0 to 169092.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.ICrop.ContainerLeft
member.summary: Gets or sets the location of the left side of a shape used to crop an image.
member.exception: Thrown if the specified value for is outside the valid range of -169092 to 169092.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.ICrop.ContainerTop
member.summary: Gets or sets the location of the top of a shape used to crop an image.
member.exception: Thrown if the specified value for is outside the valid range of -169092 to 169092.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.ICrop.ContainerWidth
member.summary: Gets or sets the width of a shape used to crop an image.
member.exception: Thrown if the specified value for is outside the valid range of 0 to 169092.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.ICrop.ContainerHeight
member.summary: Gets or sets the height of a shape used to crop an image.
member.exception: Thrown if the specified value for is outside the valid range of 0 to 169092.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStops
member.summary: Represents a collection of instances.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStops.Add
member.summary: Adds a gradient stop at the end of the collection.
member.returns: The instance added.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStops.Add(Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStop)
member.summary: Adds the specified gradient stop object at the end of the collection. A minimum of 2 gradient stops must be added when using a gradient type fill.
member.param: The gradient stop object to add to the collection.
member.returns: Returns the index of the added gradient stop.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStops.Insert(System.Int32,Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStop)
member.summary: Inserts an item into the gradient stop collection at the specified index.
member.param: The zero-based index at which the gradient stop should be inserted.
The gradient stop item to insert.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStops.RemoveAt(System.Int32)
member.summary: Removes the element at the specified index of the gradient stop collection.
member.param: The zero-based index of the element to remove.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStops.Remove(Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStop)
member.summary: Removes the first occurrence of the specified instance from the gradient stop collection.
member.param: The gradient stop object to remove from the collection.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStops.IndexOf(Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStop)
member.summary: Returns the zero-based index of the first occurrence of the instance within the collection.
member.param: The gradient stop instance to locate in the collection.
member.returns: The zero-based index of the first occurrence of the gradient stop.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStops.Clear
member.summary: Removes all the elements from the gradient stop collection.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStops.Add(Syncfusion.Drawing.Color,System.Single)
member.summary: Adds a gradient stop at the specified position. A minimum of 2 gradient stops must be added when using a gradient type fill.
member.param: The color of the gradient stop.
The position of the gradient stop within the gradient.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStops.Count
member.summary: Gets the number of elements in the gradient stop collection. Read-only.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Office.IOfficeShapeGradientStops.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets an instance of from the gradient stop collection at the specified index. Read-only.

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Office.OfficeInkEffectType
member.summary: Specifies the visual effect applied to an ink stroke in Office documents.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.OfficeInkEffectType.None
member.summary: Represents the default pen style with no special effect.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.OfficeInkEffectType.Pencil
member.summary: Applies a pencil-like effect that mimics hand-drawn graphite strokes.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.OfficeInkEffectType.Rainbow
member.summary: Applies a rainbow effect with multicolored gradients.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.OfficeInkEffectType.Galaxy
member.summary: Applies a galaxy-themed effect with a cosmic appearance.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.OfficeInkEffectType.Lava
member.summary: Applies a lava-like effect with fiery tones and textures.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.OfficeInkEffectType.Ocean
member.summary: Applies an ocean-themed effect with cool, flowing colors.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.OfficeInkEffectType.RoseGold
member.summary: Applies a rose gold metallic effect to the ink stroke.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.OfficeInkEffectType.Gold
member.summary: Applies a gold metallic effect to the ink stroke.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.OfficeInkEffectType.Silver
member.summary: Applies a silver metallic effect to the ink stroke.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Office.OfficeInkEffectType.Bronze
member.summary: Applies a bronze metallic effect to the ink stroke.

member.summary: Stores raw ink traces by their index.

member.name: M:Syncfusion.Office.OfficeInk.ConvertTraceToPoints(System.String)
member.summary: Converts the InkML trace string into a list of points.
member.remarks: The trace may contain absolute packets (!) or first/second derivatives (', "). This method walks the encoded text, applies the derivative deltas in sequence, and reconstructs the absolute numeric values for each channel sample.
